name: Traditional Hamantaschen
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve the yeast in 1/4 cup of lukewarm milk, ensuring it is not too warm.
2. Stir in 1 tablespoon of sugar and set aside.
3. In a large bowl, combine butter, remaining sugar, salt, and scalded milk. Mix until well combined.
4. When lukewarm, stir in the yeast.
5. Add eggs and about 2 cups of flour, beating to form a smooth batter.
6. Gradually add remaining flour until the dough is soft.
7. Turn out onto a floured surface; knead for approximately 2 minutes.
8. Grease a large bowl; place the dough in it, ensuring the entire surface is coated.
9. Cover and let rise at room temperature until doubled in size (approximately 2 to 4 hours).
10. Knead again on a floured surface for about a minute.
11. Roll out to 1/8 inch thickness.
12. Cut into circles of desired size.
13. Place filling in each circle according to the recipe for Mohn Filling.
14. Shape hamantaschen by pinching edges together over the filling, leaving about half open to form a cone shape.
15. Fold the excess edge and pinch firmly.
16. Arrange well apart on a greased baking sheet. Cover with a cloth.
17. Let rise again at room temperature until doubled in size.
18. Brush tops with egg yolk mixed with water.
19. Bake in a moderate oven (350°F) for 15 to 20 minutes. Makes 2 to 2 1/2 dozen.
